plan.title: Port the content validator gates (validate-links / validate_strategy_output / verify_capacity) to TypeScript

narratives.Description: Port the three content-validator gates -- scripts/validate-links.py, scripts/validate_strategy_output.py, and scripts/verify_capacity.py -- to TypeScript with cache-off golden parity vs the Python oracle. validate-links checks intra-doc link integrity, validate_strategy_output validates strategy-output structure, and verify_capacity checks the WIP/capacity policy. All are leaf gates with no inter-dependency; three-state exits are preserved. Any regex ported MUST be a linear recognizer to avoid CodeQL js/polynomial-redos while staying byte-identical.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Create packages/core/src/validate-content with TS ports of the three validators, with vitest unit tests covering clean / each-failure-class / config-error per gate.
2. Add packages/cli/src/validate-content-parity.ts that drives the TS functions and a python -c invocation of the frozen Python gates over shared fixtures and asserts byte-identical output (stdout + exit code) with cache off.
3. Wire the module into index.ts, package.json subpath exports, vitest.config.ts alias, tasks/ts.yml (validate-content-parity into parity-all), and a CI golden-diff step.
narratives.UserStory: As a directive maintainer, I want the content validator gates in TypeScript, so that the link-integrity, strategy-output, and capacity checks run on the TS engine with the same verdicts, messages, and exit codes as the Python modules.

narrative.Acceptance: Running the TS validate-content gates returns the same three-state exit code and the same diagnostic messages the Python validate-links / validate_strategy_output / verify_capacity modules produce for the clean, each-failure-class, and config-error fixtures.
Running the validate-content-parity binary returns exit 0 and renders byte-identical output to the Python content-validator oracle with cache off.

expected_outputs: New packages/core/src/validate-content module with vitest unit tests
Byte-identical parity vs the three Python content-validator gates under cache-off
swarm.missing_traces_justification: #1530 Wave-5 framework-internal TS port traced to GitHub issue #1783 and umbrella epic #1530; no specification.vbrief.json spec-section applies to maintainer tooling.

references.title: Issue #1783: feat(ts-migration): port the remaining verify/validate gates to TS (Wave 5)
